name: Asymmetric stripline with correct Height
description: Tests that the agent sets Height to a cumulative layer boundary for multi-layer stripline and does not call design() which errors on multi-layer
prompt: I need an asymmetric stripline where the signal is closer to the bottom ground. Use FR4 with 0.8mm below and 1.6mm above the signal trace. Configure it for approximately 50 ohms at 3 GHz. Show me the structure.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Uses stripLine object
Uses multi-layer dielectric with Thickness vector [0.8e-3 1.6e-3] and matching EpsilonR vector
assertions.expected: Thickness vector with two elements and matching EpsilonR vector

assertions.description: Sets Height to 0.8e-3 (first cumulative boundary), not 2.4e-3 (total)
Does not call design() which errors on multi-layer stripLine

name: Capture design() output and use getZ0 correctly
description: Tests that the agent captures design() return value and calls getZ0 without a frequency argument
prompt: Design a 50-ohm microstrip line on FR4 (er=4.4, 1.6mm thick) at 2.4 GHz. After designing, verify the characteristic impedance and print the resulting trace width.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Uses microstripLine
Captures design() output: ms = design(ms, freq) not just design(ms, freq)
assertions.expected: variable = design( pattern, not bare design() call

assertions.description: Calls getZ0 without frequency argument — getZ0(ms) not getZ0(ms, freq)
assertions.expected: getZ0(ms) or getZ0(obj) with single argument

assertions.description: Assigns metal Conductor for accurate impedance

name: RLGC extraction requires finite conductivity
description: Tests that the agent assigns metal conductor before calling rlgc and uses scalar frequency
prompt: Extract the per-unit-length RLGC parameters of a 3mm wide microstrip on 1.6mm FR4 at 5 GHz. I need L, C, R, and G values.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Calls rlgc function
Sets Conductor to metal before calling rlgc — PEC default causes error
assertions.expected: metal( assigned to Conductor before rlgc call

assertions.description: Passes scalar frequency to rlgc, not a vector

name: Differential pair with aggressor NEXT/FEXT
description: Tests that the agent uses microstripLineCustom with correct properties for differential + aggressor modeling
prompt: I have a differential pair on FR4 (0.2mm thick) and I need to measure how much crosstalk an adjacent aggressor trace causes. Use a 10mm line length and extract S-parameters up to 5 GHz.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Uses microstripLineCustom for differential + aggressor modeling
Sets TraceType to Differential
Sets LeftCoupledTraceGap or RightCoupledTraceGap to add aggressor traces
assertions.expected: CoupledTraceGap property set to nonzero value

assertions.description: Does not attempt to set GroundPlaneWidth which is read-only on microstripLineCustom
Uses TraceLength (not Length) to set the line length

name: 2D cross-section RLGC extraction
description: Tests that the agent uses pcb2D with trace2D objects for fast cross-section analysis instead of 3D sparameters
prompt: I need to quickly extract the per-unit-length RLGC parameters of a 0.3mm wide copper trace on 0.2mm FR4 at 10 GHz. I don't need full 3D simulation — just the cross-section analysis.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Uses pcb2D for fast cross-section analysis
Uses trace2D with shape.Rectangle for the trace definition
Includes a Ground type trace2D — pcb2D requires at least one ground
assertions.expected: Type = 'Ground' or Type='Ground' on a trace2D

assertions.description: Calls rlgc with scalar frequency, not vector
